title: evidence_diversity_v1 Proof of Actuation
method: Deterministic ranking comparison on controlled 3-item evidence set

controlled_evidence_set.title: High-efficiency catalyst for hydrogen evolution via platinum group reduction
controlled_evidence_set.note: High API relevance, low mechanism overlap — would rank first under champion defaults

controlled_evidence_set.title: Electrolyte interface engineering for proton exchange membrane stability
controlled_evidence_set.note: Low API relevance, high mechanism overlap — would rank first under evidence_diversity_v1

controlled_evidence_set.title: Thermal management strategies in solid oxide fuel cell systems
controlled_evidence_set.note: Medium scores across both dimensions — middle ranking in both policies

ranking_delta.rank_order_diff: Champion: A>C>B | Challenger: B>C>A — full inversion of top and bottom items

interpretation: The challenger policy (evidence_diversity_v1) meaningfully changes which evidence items are ranked highest. Items with strong mechanism alignment (ev_B) are promoted over items with strong API relevance but weak mechanism alignment (ev_A). This demonstrates that the evidence_ranking_weights change is not inert — it will surface different evidence to the candidate generator, which is the intended mechanism of action.
what_this_does_not_prove: This proof shows the policy actuates the ranking function. It does not show that the resulting candidates will be approved at a higher rate — that requires a full A/B trial with reviewer labels.
